analysis.btc_price_note: BTC opened $73,820 on Apr 20, rallied to $75,325 by ~9AM ET despite US-Iran tensions (US seized Iranian cargo ship near Strait of Hormuz). ETF inflows strong (~$1B weekly, IBIT leading). RSI 55.69 neutral. 13 bullish vs 15 bearish technicals.
sentiment_key_signals: Fear & Greed Index: 26-27 (Fear territory, slight improvement from prior days)
BTC rallying intraday from $73,820 open to $75,325 - showing resilience
Spot BTC ETF weekly inflows ~$1B (strongest since mid-Jan 2026), $663M on Apr 17 alone
US-Iran tensions: US seized Iranian cargo ship near Hormuz - risk-off headwind
Volume-to-market-cap ratio 5.8% below 7-day avg - recovery lacks full conviction
CME futures gap concern weighing on short-term sentiment
DeFi exploit (rsETH) aftermath still pressuring altcoin sentiment
No major US macro data scheduled Apr 20-21
FOMC Apr 28-29: 99% probability of hold at 3.50-3.75%

analysis.macro_backdrop: Fed funds 3.50-3.75% hold. CPI Mar 2026: +3.3% YoY. FOMC Apr 28-29 with 99% hold probability. No BTC ETF SEC decisions pending next 24h. No major economic data releases April 20-21.
analysis.technical_note: BTC holding $75K area. Key resistance $76,950 (local high) and $77,500. Support at $73,180 (immediate) and $71,000-$72,000 (200DMA). RSI 55.69 neutral. Mixed technicals (13 bullish / 15 bearish).
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral: Price recovering intraday despite geopolitical headwinds, supported by strong ETF institutional flows (~$1B weekly). However, Fear & Greed at 26 (Fear), volume below average, and mixed technicals prevent bullish call. Previous bearish regime downgraded to neutral as price reclaimed $75K. Confidence 0.40 is below 0.50 bot threshold - macro filter INACTIVE this cycle.
